title: salix × fragilis var. vitellina
intro: Salix × fragilis var. vitellina, also known as Golden Weeping Willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a variant of the Crack Willow (Salix fragilis) and is characterized by its golden-yellow bark. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Salix × fragilis var. vitellina include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
